prompt: Escalations from two tenants about the daily shipment report. New York: the report they request for 2026-03-08 comes back labeled 2026-03-07, and shipments logged in the evening keep landing on the NEXT day's report. Kolkata drifts the other way. During the US DST-change week the day windows are additionally off by an hour, and the day-length figures claim 24 hours for days that were actually 23 or 25. All of the math in dayreport.ts already receives an explicit Zone adapter (offsetMinutes east of UTC for a given instant) — the host timezone is not involved, so this reproduces on any machine: `node --test test_dayreport.ts`. One more contract point while you're in there: civil dates in this API are plain YYYY-MM-DD strings, and functions expecting a civil date must reject a full timestamp string instead of silently accepting it — we caught a caller passing an ISO instant straight through. Fix dayreport.ts; the tests are the contract and must not be touched.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of dayreport.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 3 pass / 7 fail: startOfCivilDay('2026-03-08', NEW_YORK) lands at 2026-03-07T19:00Z instead of 05:00Z, round-trip asserts report '2026-01-15 drifted to another day in New York', daily totals bucket by UTC day so evening shipments merge into the wrong date, DST day lengths come back 25/23 instead of 23/25, and timestamp strings are silently accepted as civil dates.
